examples.sentence: 試験が近いので、勉強するよりほかない。
examples.translation: Since the exam is near, there is no choice but to study.
examples.translationEn: Since the exam is near, there is no choice but to study.
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai node, benkyou suru yori hoka nai.

examples.sentence: 風邪がひどいので、早く寝るよりほかない。
examples.translation: My cold is severe, so I have no choice but to go to bed early.
examples.translationEn: My cold is severe, so I have no choice but to go to bed early.
examples.romaji: Kaze ga hidoi node, hayaku neru yori hoka nai.

examples.sentence: バスが来ないので、歩くよりほかない。
examples.translation: Since the bus isn't coming, I have no choice but to walk.
examples.translationEn: Since the bus isn't coming, I have no choice but to walk.
examples.romaji: Basu ga konai node, aruku yori hoka nai.

examples.sentence: お金がないので、安いものを買うよりほかない。
examples.translation: Since I have no money, I have no choice but to buy cheap things.
examples.translationEn: Since I have no money, I have no choice but to buy cheap things.
examples.romaji: Okane ga nai node, yasui mono o kau yori hoka nai.

examples.sentence: 彼が怪我をしたから、試合に勝てるわけがない。
examples.translation: He got injured, so there's no way he can win the match.
examples.translationEn: He got injured, so there's no way he can win the match.
examples.romaji: Kare ga kega wo shita kara, shiai ni kateru wake ga nai.

examples.sentence: 嘘ばかり言っている人を、信じるわけがない。
examples.translation: There's no way I can believe someone who constantly lies.
examples.translationEn: There's no way I can believe someone who constantly lies.
examples.romaji: Uso bakari itte iru hito wo, shinjiru wake ga nai.

examples.sentence: 彼女は忙しいから、このパーティーに来るわけがない。
examples.translation: She's busy, so there's no way she can come to this party.
examples.translationEn: She's busy, so there's no way she can come to this party.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa isogashii kara, kono paatii ni kuru wake ga nai.

examples.sentence: 昨日はずっと寝ていたから、その事件を知るわけがない。
examples.translation: I was asleep all day yesterday, so there's no way I could know about that incident.
examples.translationEn: I was asleep all day yesterday, so there's no way I could know about that incident.
examples.romaji: Kinou wa zutto nete ita kara, sono jiken wo shiru wake ga nai.

examples.sentence: 彼女が遅れたのは、電車が遅れたわけだ。
examples.translation: The reason she was late is that the train was delayed.
examples.translationEn: The reason she was late is that the train was delayed.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga okureta no wa, densha ga okureta wake da.

examples.sentence: 授業が面白かったから、みんな熱心に聞いていたわけだ。
examples.translation: The class was interesting, so that's why everyone was listening intently.
examples.translationEn: The class was interesting, so that's why everyone was listening intently.
examples.romaji: Jugyou ga omoshirokatta kara, minna nesshin ni kiiteita wake da.

examples.sentence: 彼はとても忙しいですから、休みがほとんどないわけだ。
examples.translation: He's really busy, which is why he hardly has any time off.
examples.translationEn: He's really busy, which is why he hardly has any time off.
examples.romaji: Kare wa totemo isogashii desu kara, yasumi ga hotondo nai wake da.

examples.sentence: この問題が難しいから、全然わからないわけだ。
examples.translation: This problem is tough, so no wonder I don't understand it at all.
examples.translationEn: This problem is tough, so no wonder I don't understand it at all.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai ga muzukashii kara, zenzen wakaranai wake da.

examples.sentence: 彼が勉強しないわけではないが、成績があまり良くない。
examples.translation: It's not that he doesn't study, but his grades aren't very good.
examples.translationEn: It's not that he doesn't study, but his grades aren't very good.
examples.romaji: Kare ga benkyou shinai wake dewa nai ga, seiseki ga amari yoku nai.

examples.sentence: このレストランが高いわけではないけど、毎日通うには少し高いね。
examples.translation: It's not that this restaurant is expensive, but it's a bit too pricey to go there every day.
examples.translationEn: It's not that this restaurant is expensive, but it's a bit too pricey to go there every day.
examples.romaji: Kono resutoran ga takai wake dewa nai kedo, mainichi kayou ni wa sukoshi takai ne.

examples.sentence: 彼女が優しくないわけではないが、時々話し方がきついことがある。
examples.translation: It's not that she's unkind, but sometimes her manner of speaking can be harsh.
examples.translationEn: It's not that she's unkind, but sometimes her manner of speaking can be harsh.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga yasashikunai wake dewa nai ga, tokidoki hanashikata ga kitsui koto ga aru.

examples.sentence: 彼が忙しいわけではないが、時間を上手に使えていないようだ。
examples.translation: It's not that he's busy; he just doesn't seem to use his time effectively.
examples.translationEn: It's not that he's busy; he just doesn't seem to use his time effectively.
examples.romaji: Kare ga isogashii wake dewa nai ga, jikan o jouzu ni tsukaete inai you da.

examples.sentence: 試験が近いから、遊ぶわけにはいかない。
examples.translation: Since the exam is close, I can't afford to just play around.
examples.translationEn: Since the exam is close, I can't afford to just play around.
examples.romaji: Shiken ga chikai kara, asobu wake ni wa ikanai.

examples.sentence: 田中さんが待っているので、遅れるわけにはいかない。
examples.translation: Mr. Tanaka is waiting, so I absolutely must not be late.
examples.translationEn: Mr. Tanaka is waiting, so I absolutely must not be late.
examples.romaji: Tanaka-san ga matteiru node, okureru wake ni wa ikanai.

examples.sentence: 子供たちのために、この仕事を辞めるわけにはいかない。
examples.translation: For the sake of my kids, I can't afford to quit this job.
examples.translationEn: For the sake of my kids, I can't afford to quit this job.
examples.romaji: Kodomo-tachi no tame ni, kono shigoto o yameru wake ni wa ikanai.

examples.sentence: この問題は重要だから、無視するわけにはいかない。
examples.translation: This issue is important, so I can't simply ignore it.
examples.translationEn: This issue is important, so I can't simply ignore it.
examples.romaji: Kono mondai wa juuyou dakara, mushi suru wake ni wa ikanai.

examples.sentence: 結婚をきっかけに、彼は真剣に仕事を頑張り始めた。
examples.translation: Getting married was the catalyst for him to start working seriously.
examples.translationEn: Getting married was the catalyst for him to start working seriously.
examples.romaji: Kekkon o kikkake ni, kare wa shinken ni shigoto o ganbarihajimeta.

examples.sentence: あの出会いをきっかけに、私たちは友達になりました。
examples.translation: That encounter led us to become friends.
examples.translationEn: That encounter led us to become friends.
examples.romaji: Ano deai o kikkake ni, watashitachi wa tomodachi ni narimashita.

examples.sentence: コンサートをきっかけに、彼女は音楽の道に進むことを決めました。
examples.translation: Inspired by the concert, she decided to pursue music.
examples.translationEn: Inspired by the concert, she decided to pursue music.
examples.romaji: Konsaato o kikkake ni, kanojo wa ongaku no michi ni susumu koto o kimemashita.

examples.sentence: 留学をきっかけに、私の外国語スキルが向上しました。
examples.translation: Studying abroad gave me the chance to improve my foreign language skills.
examples.translationEn: Studying abroad gave me the chance to improve my foreign language skills.
examples.romaji: Ryuugaku o kikkake ni, watashi no gaikokugo sukiru ga koujou shimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼は教師を職業として働いています。
examples.translation: He works as a teacher.
examples.translationEn: He works as a teacher.
examples.romaji: Kare wa kyoushi o shokugyou toshite hataraite imasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女はリーダーを役割として受け入れました。
examples.translation: She accepted the role of leader.
examples.translationEn: She accepted the role of leader.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa riidaa o yakuwari toshite ukeiremashita.

examples.sentence: 私たちは彼を仲間として信頼しています。
examples.translation: We trust him as one of our own (a close companion).
examples.translationEn: We trust him as one of our own (a close companion).
examples.romaji: Watashitachi wa kare o nakama toshite shinrai shite imasu.

examples.sentence: このアーティストは伝統を基盤として作品を作ります。
examples.translation: This artist creates works with tradition as the foundation.
examples.translationEn: This artist creates works with tradition as the foundation.
examples.romaji: Kono aatisuto wa dentou o kiban toshite sakuhin o tsukurimasu.

examples.sentence: このイベントは音楽を中心に楽しまれています。
examples.translation: This event is mainly enjoyed for its music.
examples.translationEn: This event is mainly enjoyed for its music.
examples.romaji: Kono ibento wa ongaku o chuushin ni tanoshimarete imasu.

examples.sentence: 彼の仕事は東京を中心に行われています。
examples.translation: His work is primarily conducted in and around Tokyo.
examples.translationEn: His work is primarily conducted in and around Tokyo.
examples.romaji: Kare no shigoto wa Toukyou o chuushin ni okonawarete imasu.

examples.sentence: 彼女は健康を中心に本を書いています。
examples.translation: She’s writing a book focusing on health.
examples.translationEn: She’s writing a book focusing on health.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa kenkou o chuushin ni hon o kaite imasu.

examples.sentence: 最近の研究は環境保護を中心に行われている。
examples.translation: Recent research is being carried out with a primary focus on environmental protection.
examples.translationEn: Recent research is being carried out with a primary focus on environmental protection.
examples.romaji: Saikin no kenkyuu wa kankyou hogo o chuushin ni okonawarete iru.
